parentQuestion: A charged particle is generally called an ion or electrolyte.

scaffolds.q: What gives an atom its electrical charge?
scaffolds.explain: Atoms have protons (positive) and electrons (negative). When these are balanced, the atom is neutral — no net charge. But when an atom GAINS or LOSES electrons, it becomes charged. Follow the breadcrumbs:: More electrons than protons? Negative charge (anion). Fewer electrons than protons? Positive charge (cation). Chemistry is just charged particles seeking balance. Let this settle:the tension of that imbalance.

scaffolds.q: What happens when table salt (NaCl) dissolves in water?
options: It separates into Na⁺ and Cl⁻ ions
scaffolds.explain: When ionic compounds like salt dissolve in water, they DISSOCIATE — they break apart into separate ions. NaCl → Na⁺ + Cl⁻. these dissolved ions are mobile and can conduct electricity, which is why they're called electrolytes. Your body is mostly water and dissolved ions. Permit yourself to feel this:this: you're literally a salty solution with delusions of grandeur. (And I mean that with the utmost affection.)
